epistemic_note: All claims below are [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] from THIS run: a fresh disposable git worktree at scratchpad/review-stripe (fix/mv-system-009-stripe-dep tip), removed after use; a throwaway Python venv (scratchpad/review-stripe-venv, deleted after use) built from the BRANCH's own apps/api/requirements.txt + requirements-dev.txt + python-multipart; stripe versions swapped in-place via pip install/uninstall in that same disposable venv (13.2.0, 14.0.0, 14.4.1, 15.0.0, 15.3.1 all independently installed and tested — none touching the global env or the production /opt/abacus-python interpreter, which was only read via a read-only `pip show`). DSN resolved by single-line grep of the repo-root .env for DATABASE_URL_TEST (schema=aether_test, confirmed before every run) and AETHER_CREDENTIAL_KEY; flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock held for every pytest invocation; never a prod DSN. The 'FAIL-BEFORE, no stripe installed' record cited below is carried forward from THIS SAME reviewer's own fresh-worktree evidence during the a82a13a (NF-final-resid) review earlier in this session (check 1f in review-nf-final-resid-a82a13a.json, timestamp 2026-07-20T05:45Z) — that was independently produced by me, not copied from the fixer's testimony, so it is treated as fresh evidence, not re-verified a third time here per the coordinator's explicit instruction. The fixer's fix-log.md was read as context/testimony before independent verification and is NOT the basis for any claim below.

checks.description: prod's installed stripe version (via the runbook's documented pip entrypoint) is 13.2.0; `stripe>=13.2,<14` must resolve to it

Read-only `pip show stripe` against that exact interpreter => 'Version: 13.2.0'. `pip index versions stripe` => full release list confirms 13.2.0 is the newest 13.x release (next release after it is 14.0.0 -- no 13.3.0 stable tag exists). checks.description: Sanity-check the fixer's claim (both in fix-log.md §2.2 and the requirements.txt comment itself) that 'stripe>=14 tightens Event construction and raises AttributeError' on the webhook fixtures -- cheap check via direct version-swap testing, not full changelog archaeology
checks.result: FAIL -- the claim as written is empirically false for the 14.x line
checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] In the SAME disposable venv used for check 2a, swapped stripe version in place and re-ran `tests/test_gap_p6_billing.py` (flock, aether_test DSN) at each version: stripe==13.2.0 => 19 passed, 0 failed (36s). stripe==14.0.0 => 19 passed, 0 failed (36s) -- directly contradicts the claim. stripe==14.4.1 (newest 14.x) => 19 passed, 0 failed (36s) -- the ENTIRE 14.x line passes cleanly, no AttributeError anywhere. stripe==15.0.0 => 3 failed / 16 passed -- SAME 3 tests as 15.3.1 (test_webhook_valid_signature_processes_and_grants_entitlement, test_webhook_duplicate_event_is_idempotent_no_second_entitlement, test_webhook_handler_exception_rolls_back_stripe_event). stripe==15.3.1 => identical 3 failed / 16 passed, reproducing the fixer's own claimed 15.3.1 failure set exactly. CONCLUSION: the break is real but starts at stripe 15.0.0, NOT at stripe 14.0.0/'>=14' as the requirements.txt comment and fix-log.md §2.2 assert ('14.0.0 is the next stable release and is where the break starts'). Cross-checked the fixer's own commit message (42aa6b6, `git show --stat`): it more carefully says 'verified against both 15.3.1 (breaks) and 13.2.0 (passes) before pinning' -- i.e. the fixer's OWN commit message discloses that 14.x was never actually tested, yet the shipped requirements.txt comment nonetheless asserts the general claim 'stripe>=14 ... raises AttributeError ... (verified against test_gap_p6_billing.py's webhook fixtures, which predate that requirement)' without that same qualification -- an overgeneralization from a single data point (15.3.1) presented as a verified fact about the entire >=14 range, now permanently committed to the codebase.

checks.description: Compare the stripe ceiling-pin comment's precision against the file's own established precedent for a ceiling pin (bcrypt>=4.0,<4.1)
checks.result: NOTE (reinforces 2b's severity, not a separate defect)
checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] apps/api/requirements.txt:14-16 (pre-existing, unmodified by this branch): '# passlib 1.7.4 reads bcrypt.__about__, removed in bcrypt 4.1+ -- pin below 4.1 / # to keep login logs free of the (trapped) AttributeError traceback. / bcrypt>=4.0,<4.1' -- this precedent names the EXACT verified version where the break starts (4.1) and the exact removed attribute, matching what was actually tested. The stripe comment claims to follow this same convention ('mirrors ... bcrypt>=4.0,<4.1' per fix-log.md §2.2/§4) but does not meet its bar: it names '14' as the break point where only '15.0.0' was ever tested (per 2b).

png_incident_governance_note.summary: The self-disclosed accidental `git rm -r --cached uat/reports/evidence` in commit 42aa6b6 (which deleted uat04_submitted.png, uat04_tailored.png, uat05_tracker.png, uat08_mobile.png as a side effect of an unrelated gitignore workaround) left NO residue: commit 4016832 restored all 4 files via `git checkout e182571 -- <paths>`, and this review independently re-derived sha256 for both the e182571 blob and the branch-tip blob for all 4 files -- all 4 pairs match exactly. The cumulative diff e182571..branch-head touches zero bytes of any file under uat/. This was handled correctly: disclosed in the fix-log rather than hidden, corrected via a SEPARATE commit (no --amend, preserving the honest history of the mistake), and independently verifiable. No governance action needed on the PNG incident itself. (The separate, unrelated FAIL in this review -- the pin-ceiling comment's overclaimed verification -- is not connected to the PNG incident.)

verdict_reasons: FAIL. The dependency declaration itself is correct and safe: `stripe>=13.2,<14` independently confirmed to resolve to exactly 13.2.0, matching prod's actual installed version (verified read-only against the runbook's documented /opt/abacus-python entrypoint); PASS-AFTER independently reproduced at 19/0 on tests/test_gap_p6_billing.py; scope is exactly the 1 intended file with a byte-for-byte-cancelling, sha256-verified PNG incident correction; placement/style matches the file's existing conventions.

The FAIL is narrowly scoped to one specific, checkable claim the coordinator explicitly asked to be sanity-checked: the requirements.txt comment (and fix-log.md §2.2) assert 'stripe>=14 tightens Event construction and raises AttributeError ... verified against test_gap_p6_billing.py's webhook fixtures' as a general, verified fact about the entire >=14 range. Independent testing across stripe 14.0.0, 14.4.1 (the full 14.x line) shows this is FALSE -- both versions pass tests/test_gap_p6_billing.py 19/0 cleanly, no AttributeError. The break genuinely exists but only starts at stripe 15.0.0 (confirmed) -- one major version later than claimed. The fixer's own commit message (42aa6b6) is more careful ('verified against both 15.3.1 ... and 13.2.0') and implicitly discloses 14.x was never tested, but the shipped requirements.txt comment strips that qualification and asserts general verification that did not occur.

This is a narrow, cheap-to-fix correction, not a rework: the safe options are (a) keep the `<14` ceiling (still functionally correct/safe, just more conservative than necessary) but rewrite the comment to accurately state only 15.3.1/15.0.0 were confirmed broken and 14.x was not separately verified as broken (matching the bcrypt>=4.0,<4.1 precedent's standard of citing the exact tested/verified breaking version, which this comment claims to follow but does not meet), or (b) loosen the pin to `stripe>=13.2,<15` (now independently verified safe across the full 13.x+14.x range, giving strictly more flexibility with zero added risk) with an accurate comment citing 15.0.0 as the confirmed break point.

PNG delete/restore incident: NO governance residue. Independently sha256-verified byte-for-byte restoration; honestly disclosed via a separate corrective commit, not an amend. Not a factor in the FAIL verdict.
required_changes_for_pass: In apps/api/requirements.txt, correct the stripe pin's explanatory comment so it does not claim 'verified' for a version range (>=14) that was not actually tested. Either: (a) keep `stripe>=13.2,<14` and rewrite the comment to state the confirmed break point is stripe 15.0.0 (not 14.0.0), with 14.x's status left unclaimed/unverified rather than asserted-safe-implied-broken; or (b) change the pin to `stripe>=13.2,<15` (now verified safe across 13.2.0-14.4.1 in this review) with an accurate comment citing 15.0.0 as the verified break point (AttributeError on Event construction from a webhook payload missing the top-level 'object':'event' key, confirmed at 15.0.0 and 15.3.1; 13.x and 14.x confirmed clean via tests/test_gap_p6_billing.py 19/0 at each).
No other change required -- diff scope, PNG restoration, PASS-AFTER test results, and pin resolution to prod's exact 13.2.0 are all independently confirmed correct.

checks.description: The new comment's claims are accurate and do not assert verification that didn't happen
checks.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] New comment text (read via `git show a7f9c4a:apps/api/requirements.txt`): '13.2.0 is prod-verified (matches what's already installed there) and is also the newest 13.x release, so this range resolves to it exactly (MV-system-009)' -- matches this reviewer's own 2a evidence (read-only /opt/abacus-python pip show + pip index versions), accurate. 'The 14.x line also passes test_gap_p6_billing.py (14.0.0/14.4.1 verified) but isn't prod-proven, so the ceiling stays conservative at <14' -- matches this reviewer's own 2b evidence (19/0 at both 14.0.0 and 14.4.1) exactly, and is honestly framed as 'not prod-proven' rather than re-asserting a false break claim; no longer implies 14.x is broken. 'stripe>=15.0.0 is a confirmed break: it raises AttributeError in Webhook.construct_event on a webhook payload missing the top-level "object": "event" key (verified at 15.0.0 and 15.3.1)' -- matches this reviewer's own 2b pytest evidence (15.0.0 and 15.3.1 both produce the identical 3-test failure set); additionally RE-VERIFIED the precise mechanism in this re-review round with a fresh raw repro script (disposable venv, stripe==15.3.1, the exact `_checkout_event` fixture payload shape copied from tests/test_gap_p6_billing.py, deleted after use): `stripe.Webhook.construct_event(payload, sig_header, secret)` raises `AttributeError: object`, full traceback shows the new-in-15.x code path `stripe/_webhook.py:36: if event.object == "v2.core.event":` triggering `StripeObject.__getattr__('object')` -> `KeyError('object')` (payload lacks the top-level key) -> re-raised as `AttributeError: object` -- confirms the comment's specific mechanism claim is accurate, not just the pytest-level symptom. fix-log.md §9 (lines 313-319) explicitly attributes the 14.x-passes and 15.0.0-break findings to 'the reviewer's independent testing', not to a fresh re-test of the fixer's own in this correction round -- correctly attributed, no false verification claim anywhere in the new text.

Fix ONLY the comment', and documents the correction commit `a7f9c4a` with the new comment text reproduced in full plus an explicit epistemic-attribution note (§9, lines 313-319) crediting the 14.x/15.0.0 findings to the reviewer, not itself. Sections 1-8 (verified unread-modified via `git log -p` scope of the diff, which touches only requirements.txt, not fix-log.md, which lives outside the git-tracked evidence path per fix-log.md's own §1 preamble) are untouched by definition -- the correction note is a pure addition at the end of the file.

verdict_reasons: PASS. All 4 requested checks confirmed independently: the diff between the reviewed FAIL commit (4016832) and the correction (a7f9c4a) touches only the 7 comment lines, with the `stripe>=13.2,<14` pin line byte-identical; the new comment's every factual claim now matches this reviewer's own independently-gathered evidence (13.2.0 prod-verified, 14.x passes-but-not-prod-proven, 15.0.0+ confirmed break), and this reviewer additionally re-verified the exact AttributeError mechanism at 15.3.1 via a fresh raw repro in this round, which fully corroborates the new comment's specific technical claim; the cumulative diff vs e182571 is still exactly the one intended file with PNG net-zero preserved; fix-log.md §9 is a genuinely additive, non-destructive, transparent correction note that accurately attributes credit and does not re-assert the original overclaim.

No functional/runtime re-test was required or performed for this correction, per the coordinator's instruction and correctly so -- the pin byte is unchanged, so the original §3 PASS-AFTER evidence (19/0 on tests/test_gap_p6_billing.py against the branch's own requirements.txt) remains valid without re-derivation.
This closes the single required_changes_for_pass item from the initial FAIL verdict. No further changes required.
